context.description: A reflective prose passage argues that emotions shape self-image, perception of reality, motivation, and the stories people tell about themselves. It uses an example of obesity and a potential heart attack to illustrate how pain, strain, and mortality can trigger change.
normalization_notes: Source language is German; summary translated to English.
No explicit chapter title or number is visible.

rows.paragraph_text: Emotions help form self-image and determine how reality, past, future, and priorities are perceived.
rows.text_content: Emotions help form self-image and determine how reality, past, future, and priorities are perceived.

rows.paragraph_text: They drive motivation and action even when inner ideals and lived reality conflict.
rows.text_content: They drive motivation and action even when inner ideals and lived reality conflict.

rows.paragraph_text: An example of obesity and a possible heart attack shows how pain and fear can eventually create the will to change.
rows.text_content: An example of obesity and a possible heart attack shows how pain and fear can eventually create the will to change.
